definitions.definition: The Olympic Movement is a worldwide movement of amateur athletes and sports organizations that promotes the ideals of fair play, friendship, and respect among nations. It was founded by Pierre de Coubertin in 1894 and is based on the ancient Olympic Games. The movement aims to promote physical education, sports development, and international friendship through sports.
definitions.example: The Olympic Movement has played a significant role in promoting unity and peace among nations through sports.

definitions.definition: The International Olympic Committee (IOC) is the supreme governing body of the Olympic Movement. It was founded in 1894 and is responsible for organizing the Olympic Games, promoting Olympic ideals, and supervising Olympic activities worldwide.
definitions.example: The IOC has been instrumental in promoting the Olympic Movement and its ideals globally.

definitions.definition: The Olympic Charter is the official document of the Olympic Movement, which outlines the principles, values, and rules of the Olympic Games. It was first adopted in 1894 and has been revised several times since then.
definitions.example: The Olympic Charter emphasizes the importance of fair play, respect, and friendship among athletes and nations.

definitions.definition: The Olympic Games are a major international multi-sport event held every four years, where athletes from around the world compete in various sports. The modern Olympic Games were revived in 1896 and have been held every four years since then.
definitions.example: The Olympic Games are a powerful symbol of international unity and friendship through sports.

definitions.definition: The Olympic Oath is a promise made by athletes, officials, and judges to respect the rules and spirit of the Olympic Games. It is taken before the start of each Olympic Games.
definitions.example: The Olympic Oath emphasizes the importance of fair play and respect among athletes and officials.

definitions.definition: The Olympic Flag is a symbol of the Olympic Movement, featuring five interconnected rings of equal size, colored blue, yellow, black, green, and red. The flag was designed by Pierre de Coubertin in 1913.
definitions.example: The Olympic Flag is a powerful symbol of international unity and friendship through sports.

definitions.definition: The Olympic Creed is a statement of the Olympic ideals, which emphasizes the importance of fair play, friendship, and respect among athletes and nations.
definitions.example: The Olympic Creed was first adopted in 1920 and has been a guiding principle of the Olympic Movement ever since.

definitions.definition: The Paralympic Games are a major international multi-sport event held every four years, where athletes with physical disabilities compete in various sports. The Paralympic Games were first held in 1960.
definitions.example: The Paralympic Games promote the inclusion and participation of athletes with disabilities in sports.

definitions.definition: The Youth Olympic Games are a major international multi-sport event held every four years, where young athletes aged 14-18 from around the world compete in various sports. The Youth Olympic Games were first held in 2010.
definitions.example: The Youth Olympic Games promote the development of young athletes and the spread of Olympic values among youth.

definitions.definition: The Olympic Village is a residential area built for athletes during the Olympic Games, providing accommodation, dining, and recreational facilities.
definitions.example: The Olympic Village is a symbol of international unity and friendship among athletes.

definitions.definition: The Olympic Torch Relay is a tradition where the Olympic flame is carried from Olympia, Greece to the host city of the Olympic Games, symbolizing the connection between the ancient and modern Olympic Games.
definitions.example: The Olympic Torch Relay is a powerful symbol of international unity and friendship through sports.

definitions.definition: The Olympic Flame is a symbol of the Olympic Movement, which is lit at the Olympic Stadium during the opening ceremony of each Olympic Games.
definitions.example: The Olympic Flame is a powerful symbol of international unity and friendship through sports.

definitions.definition: A National Olympic Committee (NOC) is a national organization responsible for promoting the Olympic Movement and participating in the Olympic Games within a country.
definitions.example: The Indian Olympic Association is the National Olympic Committee of India.

definitions.definition: The International Olympic Committee (IOC) Session is a meeting of the IOC, where important decisions are made regarding the Olympic Movement, including the election of IOC members and the selection of host cities for the Olympic Games.
definitions.example: The IOC Session is a significant event in the Olympic Movement, where key decisions are made regarding the future of the Olympic Games.

definitions.definition: The Olympic Programme is a list of sports and events that will be featured at the Olympic Games, which is approved by the IOC.
definitions.example: The Olympic Programme is reviewed and updated by the IOC every four years to reflect changes in sports and competitions.

definitions.definition: Olympic Sports are sports that are featured at the Olympic Games, which are approved by the IOC.
definitions.example: The IOC has added several new sports to the Olympic Programme in recent years, including surfing and skateboarding.

definitions.definition: Olympic Values are the principles and ideals that guide the Olympic Movement, including respect, friendship, and fair play.
definitions.example: The Olympic Values are an important part of the Olympic Movement, promoting international unity and friendship through sports.

definitions.definition: Olympic Education is a program that promotes the Olympic values and ideals through education, which is an important part of the Olympic Movement.
definitions.example: Olympic Education promotes the development of young athletes and the spread of Olympic values among youth.
